paper_metadata.title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
paper_metadata.abstract: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces parameter-sharing across layers and factorized embedding parameterization, reducing model size while maintaining performance. It replaces BERT's Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) with Sentence-order Prediction (SOP) to better model inter-sentence coherence.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Not explicitly stated in the provided text, but based on context, likely includes large-scale text corpora such as Wikipedia and BookCorpus
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, text classification
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces factorized embedding parameterization, which decouples the embedding size from the hidden size, and cross-layer parameter sharing, where all parameters are shared across transformer layers to improve parameter efficiency.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked language modeling (MLM), Sentence ordering objective

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT-xxlarge uses parameter sharing across layers and factorized embedding parameterization, reducing model size while maintaining performance. It uses a lightweight architecture with shared parameters to improve efficiency.
